tldr: Aider is a terminal-based AI pair programmer that edits local files via CLI, while Bolt.new is a browser-based AI full-stack environment that scaffolds and runs apps from prompts. Migration means moving from a CLI-driven workflow against your own repo to a hosted in-browser IDE.
why_switch: Run, preview, and deploy from the browser
export_method: Aider operates against a local Git repository, so export is mostly already done: your code lives in your repo and version history. Capture your Aider configuration file (commonly a project-level config plus environment variables for the model provider), any wrapper scripts, and your prompt templates. If you keep transcripts of Aider sessions, archive them locally. Note which model backends you use (OpenAI, Anthropic, etc.) and your API key sources. Document conventions like commit-on-edit behavior and any pre-commit hooks Aider relied on, since those will need a different home in a hosted environment.
import_method: In Bolt.new, start by importing or pasting your existing project, or scaffold a fresh app and copy code in. The browser IDE handles dependencies, runtime, and preview, so you typically configure the project structure and let it install. Wire up environment variables in the project settings for any external API keys you previously fed Aider. Recreate prompt templates as in-IDE instructions or saved snippets. For team workflows, connect Bolt.new to your Git remote so changes flow back to your canonical repo. Validate that your test suite and scripts still run inside the hosted runtime, and adjust commands that assumed a local terminal environment.
gotchas: Bolt.new runs in a hosted browser environment, so workflows that assume local filesystem or system tools may need adaptation
Aider works against any LLM backend you configure; Bolt.new's available models depend on the platform
Long-running processes or specialized native dependencies may not behave the same in a browser runtime
Aider commits directly to Git; verify Bolt.new's commit / sync model matches your team's expectations
The Bolt.new repo health signals show low recent activity, so monitor product direction
Cost models differ: Aider is OSS with user-paid model usage, while Bolt.new bundles tiers

email.subject: Dev workflow change: moving from Aider to Bolt.new over 14 days
email.body:
Hi there,

A quick heads up that over the next two weeks our team will be moving its AI-assisted development workflow from Aider to Bolt.new. The motivation is to reduce local setup overhead and let more of the team collaborate on prototypes directly in the browser.

What this means for you:
- Nothing changes in the product you use day-to-day; this is an internal tooling change.
- Code review and release cadence stay the same.
- You may see slightly faster iteration on small features as we settle in.

If you notice anything unusual in releases during this period, just reply and we'll investigate.

Thanks for being with us.

Your customer success team
